name: Grade 7 Homestead Lab — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 7 pathway for evaluating evidence, refining methods, and communicating an argument through Homestead Lab. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 7 knowledge and transferable skills in evaluating evidence, refining methods, and communicating an argument through Homestead Lab.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: evaluating evidence, refining methods, and communicating an argument through Homestead Lab
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about evaluating evidence, refining methods, and communicating an argument through Homestead Lab and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about evaluating evidence, refining methods, and communicating an argument through Homestead Lab, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 7 task connected to evaluating evidence, refining methods, and communicating an argument through Homestead Lab.
The learner uses relevant evidence, vocabulary, or a model to explain reasoning.
The learner revises, seeks feedback, or identifies a specific next step.
